title: Critical reaction in Japan
wikitext:
[]: Critical reaction in Japan?             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 7:01 am So, I've been wondering. What kind of critical reaction have the movies received in Japan? I am of course aware what a massive success the second movie was over there. I have no doubt that the general public loves it to bits. But what about the actual critics? Have they brought up the same issues that we bicker over?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Mar 14, 2011 7:45 am [URL=http://img571.imageshack.us/i/zszsep10eva20.jpg/] [/URL] IIRC on 2chan the practically only nigh-endless complaint thread is not surprisingly treatment of Asuka in second film. It doesn't look like there are big differences between West and East it seems

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Mar 14, 2011 11:54 am Here's a review by a professional Japanese critic I found a while back. He gave the film a score of 85/100. The same critic gave Bay's first Transformers movie an 80/100 and the second a 90/100, so Evangelion 2.0 is apparently halfway between the two Transformers movies in terms of quality.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 7:50 pm ^WOW, what crazy standards that guy must have what? did Eva 2.0 not have enough implied anatomy on the Evas themselves compared to the Transformers (VAGINA PALMS, man, VAGINA PALMS), or was the scene with Kaji and Shinji not uncomfortable enough compared to the apparently racist depictions of some Transformers?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:00 pm NAveryW wrote: Here's a review by a professional Japanese critic I found a while back. He gave the film a score of 85/100. The same critic gave Bay's first Transformers movie an 80/100 and the second a 90/100, so Evangelion 2.0 is apparently halfway between the two Transformers movies in terms of quality.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:05 pm It's likely that the "humor" of the second Transformers film was lost on him, whether he watched it in dub or sub. That being said, sounds like a guy who grades movies by the number of explosions they contain.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:08 pm Big budget Hollywood films probably impress some Japanese people more than Americans.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:09 pm drinian wrote: It's likely that the "humor" of the second Transformers film was lost on him, whether he watched it in dub or sub.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:25 pm drinian wrote: That being said, sounds like a guy who grades movies by the number of explosions they contain.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Mar 14, 2011 10:19 pm Big budget, high concept special effects extravaganzas seem to be received better in general overseas than in the United States. Perhaps it's more impressive because it's the sort of thing that only Hollywood has the spending power to pull off. For comparison, here are the ratings from some of his other reviews: The King's Speech : 75/100 Gantz : 45/100 (Sankaku Complex translated excerpts of his review if you wish to read it) Tron Legacy : 65/100 Shrek 4 : 30/100 Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows Part I : 65/100 Eat, Pray, Love : 30/100 The Sorcerer's Apprentice : 65/100 How to Train Your Dragon : 97/100 Toy Story 3 : 95/100 Iron Man 2 : 65/100 Sex and the City 2 : 65/100 Clash of the Titans : 75/100

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Mar 14, 2011 10:35 pm He seems to have a level head on his shoulders. I agree with most of those ratings, though I wouldn't know about his sentiments on the films seeing as how I can't read Japanese. And yeah. I guess we're spoiled in the States for being the only nation with visual effects that good and yet still be bored by them. It still doesn't excuse select special effect extravaganzas for not having a well written script, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Mar 14, 2011 10:38 pm This thread seems incredibly off-topic. Focusing on one dude instead of the community as a whole. Anywho, far as I know the Japanese critical community has been very kind to Rebuild. But I am curious if anyone has a concrete answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 14, 2011 11:34 pm The only thing I know is Japanese reviews of 2.0--on say Amazon.co.jp--mostly agree that the movie is brilliant or enjoy it greatly; a very small minority dislike it for whatever reason. (Obviously what folks on Amazon.co.jp think doesn't speak for Japan as a whole, it's just one example of people's thoughts.) 329 Reviews 5 star: (232) 4 star: (38) 3 star: (26) 2 star: (18) 1 star: (15) Link (Blu-Ray) Here . Any actual reviews from a critic, I have no clue what they think unfortunately.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Mar 15, 2011 12:55 am Alien vs. Predator got an 80/100 from him; Ponyo got a 55. He gave all the Pokémon movies he reviewed 60/100, so the Pokémon movies are better than Ponyo but not as good as AvP. I'm pretty curious now if this guy represents the average Japanese film critic.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Mar 15, 2011 1:54 am This thread needs seriously more the fantastic reviewers of Japan Times like Mark Schilling and Kaori Shoji - too bad as far as I can tell they never reviewed new films I'd like to know what Tadao Sato thinks too, if he has seen them
summary:
[]: Critical reaction in Japan?             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 7:01 am So, I've been wondering. What kind of critical reaction have the movies received in Japan? I am of course aware what a massive success the second movie was over there. I have no doubt that the general public loves it to bits. But what about the actual critics? Have they brought up the same issues that we bicker over?

[]:    &n
